title: Yerevan Music Week: Dark Jazz Concert
venue: State Philharmonia of Armenia Ground Floor

narratives.full_description:
Concert by Mard Mutantar — unfolded as a ritual in sound. A research-driven project born at Notations Lab

Mard Mutantar unfolded as a ritual in sound — a research-driven project born at Notations Lab #1, where improvisation turned into sacrament.

Yerevan Music Week: Dark Jazz Concert 
 Yerevan Music Week: Dark Jazz Concert 
 Concert by Mard Mutantar 
 
 At Yerevan Music Week , Mard Mutantar unfolded as a ritual in sound — a research-driven project born at Notations Lab #1, where improvisation turned into sacrament. The performance traced beauty in solitude and nostalgia, finding light by embracing shadow. 
 
 On stage, voices, instruments & visuals from distant worlds came together: 
 
 Lerk — voice 
 Tigran Suchyan (Toti) — trumpet 
 Abgar Alaverdyan — bass 
 Hamlet Grigoryan — drums 
 Andriesh Gandrabur — guitar 
 Hovhannes Hovhannisyan — cello 
 Nikola Chen — Electronica 
 Vardan Harutyunyan VHS — keyboards 
 Mileta Ghazanchyan — Synths 
 Lusine MLKE-Galstyan — Flute, voice 
 Gevorg Grigoryan — Visuals 
 
 
 Partners 
 Partners 
 
 The State Philharmonia of Armenia 
 https://babajanyanhall.org/en/ 
 
The State Philharmonia of Armenia has been  established in 1930. Throughout its activity, Philharmonia has played a significant role in preserving and developing Armenian musical culture. International educational and cognitive projects, concerts, festivals, competitions, master classes, presentations are organised by the institution. Philharmonia is also engaged in the production of scientific-cognitive films and publishing. The mission of Philharmonia is to contribute to the development of the country's cultural sector by presenting the best works of Armenian and world art.
